Pursuant to our discussion today, I would appreciate 
a written opinion from you regarding the following question: 
Can a campaign committee or a campaign committee 
member approve or authorize expenditures, or does the sole 
authority rest with the campaign treasurer? 
Thank you for expediting this matter.

sections.question: Can a campaign committee or a campaign committee member approve or authorize expenditures, or does the sole authority rest with the campaign treasurer?
sections.conclusion: Every committee shall have a treasurer. No expenditure shall be made by or on behalf of a committee without the authorization of the treasurer or that of his or her designated agents. No contribution or expenditure shall be accepted or made on or behalf of a committee at any time when there is a vacancy in the office of treasurer. Although the duty of authorizing expenditures may be delegated to designated agents, the treasurer must maintain detailed records per Government Code Section 84104.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether campaign committee members or the full committee can authorize expenditures, or if this authority belongs exclusively to the campaign treasurer.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under Government Code Section 84100, only the treasurer or their designated agents can authorize committee expenditures; no other committee members have this authority. The treasurer cannot delegate this responsibility when the office is vacant and must maintain detailed accounting records.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document quality is poor with significant OCR errors and missing text. Both the incoming request (June 23, 1980) and FPPC response (June 30, 1980) are included. Question and conclusion extracted despite OCR degradation. Confidence slightly reduced due to text quality but core information is recoverable.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can a campaign committee or a campaign committee member approve or authorize expenditures, or does the sole authority rest with the campaign treasurer?

CONCLUSION: Every committee shall have a treasurer. No expenditure shall be made by or on behalf of a committee without the authorization of the treasurer or that of his or her designated agents. No contribution or expenditure shall be accepted or made on or behalf of a committee at any time when there is a vacancy in the office of treasurer. Although the duty of authorizing expenditures may be delegated to designated agents, the treasurer must maintain detailed records per Government Code Section 84104.

Box 807 Sacramento, CA 95804 Dear Mr. Sobel: COMMITTEES: BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CHAIRMAN AGRICULTURE AND WATER RESOURCES HEALTH AND WELFARE MEMBER; COMMISSION OF THE CALIFORNIAS Pursuant to our discussion today, I would appreciate a written opinion from you regarding the following question: Can a campaign committee or a campaign committee member approve or authorize expenditures, or does the sole authority rest with the campaign treasurer? Thank you for expediting this matter. APG:sr f3
embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter to Senator Garcia clarifying that campaign committee treasurers have sole authority to approve expenditures under Government Code Section 84100, and may only delegate this authority to designated agents.
